mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or Rank IC > 0.05 in inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal a tightening cycle with stagflation risks, causing equity market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in > 50% of cases where both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 3.0%, or interaction effect size < 0.1 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp monthly decline in UNRATE signals tightening labor markets, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where UNRATE declines by > 0.5pp, or t-test p > 0.1 for the signal's effect.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates stronger corporate capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO's 5d return correlation drops below 0.15, or SPX shows negative returns in > 50% of INDPRO upturns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days through sectoral spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in > 40% of HOUST growth periods, or Rank IC < 0.05 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales (RSAFS) signal weak consumer spending, causing S&P 500 declines over 60 days through reduced corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in > 50% of RSAFS decline periods, or t-test p > 0.05 for the signal's effect.

mechanism_narrative: A 100k+ monthly increase in PAYEMS indicates robust job market growth, driving S&P 500 positive returns over 5 days through higher consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of PAYEMS growth instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 in rolling windows.
